ocr.text: The data presented in the table offers a nuanced examination of the relationship between ethnicity, depression, and adherence to
treatment among various populations. Specifically, it highlights the prevalence of depression among different ethnic groups, as
well as their corresponding levels of adherence and non-adherence to prescribed treatment regimens. For instance, within the
White/Mixed ethnic category, the table indicates that 125 individuals, or 94%, reported no depressive symptoms, while 8
individuals, accounting for 6%, exhibited non-adherence to treatment. This data is critical for understanding how ethnicity may
influence mental health outcomes and treatment compliance.
Variable  Adherence
Ethinicity  Depression  Adherence  non adherence  Total
White/Mixed  w/o depression  125 (94%)  8 (6%)  133
Black  w/o depression  11 (84.6%)  2 (15.4%)  13

At least one domain scored unable to do at all  237  195,924  1.0 (0.82, 1.15)
At least one domain scored a lot of difficulty/unable to do, or, at least some difficulty scored in two domains  2802  2,387,716  11.8 (11.15, 12.53)
ß  Standard Error  Wald Test  p  OR (95% CI)
Dependent Variables
Alcohol-related problems  0.26  0.09  7.83  0.005  1.29 (1.08–1.55)
Furthermore, the inclusion of metrics such as the estimated population and the prevalence percentage provides a broader
context for interpreting these results. The estimated population of individuals represented in this category is substantial, totaling
over 5.2 million, which underscores the importance of tailoring mental health interventions to address specific demographic
needs. The analysis of these metrics is essential for informing public health strategies and improving adherence rates in diverse
populations.
